pair.missing_credentials: Please enter your Google OAuth Client ID and Client Secret in the app settings first (More > Apps > Google Health > Configure app).
pair.not_authorized: Google login was not completed. Please try again.
pair.wrong_account: This login belongs to a different Google account than this device. Please sign in with the original account.

device.auth_lost: Google login expired. Open the device and use 'Repair' to log in again.
device.missing_scopes: No Google permission for: __types__. Use 'Repair' and tick ALL checkboxes on the Google consent screen.
device.api_disabled: The Google Health API is disabled for your Cloud project. Enable it in the Google Cloud Console.
device.sync_failing: Repeated sync errors for: __types__. Check the app log for details.

settings.title: Google Health
settings.intro: This app needs its own Google Cloud OAuth client, because Google grants Health API access per project.
settings.step1: Open the Google Cloud Console and create (or select) a project.
settings.step2: Enable the Google Health API for the project.
settings.step3: Create an OAuth client ID of type 'Web application' and add this authorized redirect URI:
settings.step4: While the OAuth consent screen is in 'Testing' mode, add your own Google account as a test user. Note: in testing mode Google expires logins after 7 days — publish the consent screen for permanent access.
settings.step5: Paste the Client ID and Client Secret below, save, then add the device. Important: on the Google sign-in screen, tick ALL permission checkboxes — Google leaves them unchecked by default.
settings.enable_write: Allow writing to Google Health (needed for the 'Log weight' Flow card)
settings.enable_write_hint: After changing this, run 'Repair' on existing devices (or pair a new one) to apply the new permission.
settings.enable_cardiac: Enable ECG and irregular heart rhythm data (extra Google permissions)
settings.enable_cardiac_hint: Adds the ECG and irregular-rhythm scopes for the matching flow cards. After changing this, run 'Repair' on the device and tick the new permissions on Google's screen.

report.title: Health report
report.intro: Generate a printable overview of your health data — e.g. to show your doctor. Use your browser's print dialog to save it as a PDF.
report.height_hint: Leave empty to use the height stored in Google Health.
report.inline_ready: Report generated below. Use 'Open in browser' to print or save it as a PDF (your device must be on the same network as your Homey).
report.access_info: Password: __password__ — any username, link valid for __minutes__ minutes.
report.customize_hint: Tick what to include — unticked items won't be printed or saved to the PDF.
report.disclaimer: Generated by Health Sync for Google Health (Homey) from your Google Health data. This is not a medical document; values come from consumer devices and may be inaccurate. Not affiliated with Google.
report.hint: The report appears below on this page. 'Open in browser' serves it briefly from your Homey on the local network — open it there and print or save as PDF.
